b1.h1: Every Project in Romania Looks Investment-Ready.
b1.subheadline: Technical Validation Shows a Different Picture.
b1.heroBody: A project's investment value lies not in its documents — but in its grid connectivity and the true integrity of its permit chain. AFA Energy makes these layers visible before the investment decision, through the ATR Matrix™ methodology.

b2.paragraph1: AFA Energy is an expert company based in Istanbul and Bucharest, providing technical assessment and advisory services in the Romanian and Turkish renewable energy markets.
b2.paragraph2: Through the ATR Matrix™ methodology, we select only projects with solid technical fundamentals from those we examine and connect them with institutional investors. The bond between developer and investor is the product of our selection discipline.

statusCards.name: Proceed
statusCards.desc: Becomes visible on the investor side

statusCards.name: Conditional
statusCards.desc: Can be evaluated with defined risk conditions

statusCards.name: Blocking Findings
statusCards.desc: Does not become visible on the investor side

b2.footnote: AFA preliminary technical assessment covers analysis of ATR documents and examination of grid connection conditions.
b2.pullQuoteSub: AFA brings project uncertainty to a measurable ground.

b3.h2: Every Project Is Examined on Four Axes.
b3.intro: AFA subjects every project it examines to the same methodological discipline before the investment decision. The project phase may change, but the framework does not.
b3.ctaBandH2: Evaluate your projects with ATR Matrix™
b3.ctaBandBody: Identify technical risks at an early stage with our 4-axis analysis methodology and strengthen your investment decisions.

cards.title: Connection Applicability
cards.body: The actual applicability of ATR documents and connection conditions is examined. Gaps between documentation and field reality are identified.

cards.title: Grid Capacity and Production Curtailment Risk
cards.body: Regional grid load, existing capacity, and production curtailment probability are assessed. Curtailment risk is made visible before the investment decision.

cards.title: Connection Cost and Grid Investment
cards.body: Connection fees, additional grid works, and possible reinforcement costs are examined. Hidden CAPEX risks that may arise post-ATR are made visible at an early stage.

cards.title: Permit Chain and Schedule Risk
cards.body: The project's permit chain and regulatory processes are assessed for chronological consistency. The impact of missing permits and procedural gaps on the investment schedule is analyzed.

banners.title: ATR Matrix™ — Grid Connection Analysis
banners.desc: The financial impact of connection conditions is made visible at an early stage; uncertainty transforms into an assessment ground usable in the investment decision.

banners.title: Investment Committee-Ready Outputs
banners.desc: Each finding is defined, classified, and structured to strengthen the decision ground. Outputs are prepared with format and traceability logic suitable for investment committee assessment.

banners.title: Turkey–Romania Bridge
banners.desc: Different grid structures and regulatory frameworks are addressed together within a comparable analysis framework, providing consistent comparison and predictable decision process in both markets.

banners.title: Bankability Analysis
banners.desc: The analysis and reporting structure is designed to align with technical due diligence expectations based on a bankability perspective.

banners.title: Selectivity Is a Value Discipline
banners.desc: Projects with high uncertainty and limited maturity are filtered before becoming visible on the investor side; the presented project pool is maintained at a consistent maturity level.

b5.intro: Romania and Turkey produce different types of risk due to different grid structures and regulatory dynamics. The same project type is evaluated with different sources of uncertainty in two markets. AFA's approach focuses on making these risks visible in a comparable context suitable for investment committee assessment.

markets.name: Romania
markets.leftBody: The ATR document grants a theoretical connection right; however, actual grid capacity and production curtailment risk often present a different picture from what appears in the document.
markets.rightBody: An uncertainty band forms over revenue visibility, schedule realism, and bankability. This uncertainty directly affects which risk is assumed under which conditions.

markets.name: Turkey
markets.leftBody: Although the regulatory framework appears generally stable, implementation can vary at the Electricity Distribution Company level. These differences become more pronounced in areas such as the divergence between theoretical capacity and actual connection reality, permit chain inconsistencies, and the predictability of regional grid constraints.
markets.rightBody: Implementation differences can widen the uncertainty band on cash flow reliability through project schedule, permit chain, and contractual risk allocation.

b5.closingParagraph1: The same project title produces different decision discussions in two markets due to different sources of uncertainty. Reading market dynamics in a comparable framework prepares the ground for the investment committee to distinguish these differences at an early stage.
b5.closingParagraph2: This Romania-centered analysis framework lays the groundwork for an expanding scope toward developing renewable energy markets in Central and Eastern Europe.

b6b7.subtitle:
Right project. Right investor.
Solid technical ground.
b6b7.body: For investors seeking access to technically validated projects in Romania — or for developers wanting to initiate the assessment process for their project — this is the right place.
b6b7.disclaimer: AFA's scope covers preliminary technical assessment and process coordination. Prior to project-specific final decisions, independent legal due diligence and required technical verifications should be conducted separately.
